questionHtml:
(a) admits (b) pleases (c) advances (d) accompanies Sol.323. (b) done to Cloze Test Past participle is the third form of the verb and denotes a completed action. It is used to describe a noun to provide additional information about it. Similarly, in the given passage, it provides additional information about the noun ‘harm’. Hence, ‘done(V3) to’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.324. (c) routine ‘Routine’ means a sequence of actions regularly performed. The given passage talks about to progress on routine vaccinations. Hence, ‘routine’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.325. (b) has emerged The given passage is written in the present tense and is talking about the consequences of the pandemic in the present. So, we will use a verb of present tense. ‘Singular Sub + has + V3’ is the correct structure of the present perfect tense. Hence, ‘has emerged(V3)’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.326. (b) access to ‘Access’ means the state of being able to use something. The given passage states that keeping aside the lopsided access to vaccines, learning losses have been greatest among poorest children. Hence, ‘access to’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.327. (c) foremost ‘Foremost’ means more important than anything else. The given passage states that prospects for children continue to hinge foremost on the pandemic and how it is managed. Hence, 'foremost' is the most appropriate answer. Sol.328. (d) alterations ‘Alteration’ means the process of changing or altering something. The given passage states that alterations in rainfall and plant distribution affect the migration patterns, feeding behaviour, and breeding preferences of numerous bird species. Hence, ‘alterations’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.329. (c) marine ‘Marine’ means found in the sea. The given passage states that the increase in the temperature may also alter ocean currents, which may alter the marine ecology as a whole. Hence, ‘marine’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.330. (c) vicious ‘Vicious loop’ means a continuing unpleasant situation, created when one problem causes another problem that then makes the first problem worse. The given passage states that this will result in a vicious loop. Hence, ‘vicious’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.331. (d) accelerate ‘Accelerate’ means increase in rate, amount, or extent. The given passage states that the additional emissions may accelerate the global warming process. Hence, 'accelerate' is the most appropriate answer. Sol.332. (b) extinction ‘Extinction’ means a situation in which something no longer exists. The given passage states that this may offer us hope that we will be able to leave a green planet to our future generations, who presently appear to face extinction. Hence, 'extinction' is the most appropriate answer. Sol.333. (b) eating The given passage states that Paul was eating his supper. Hence ‘eating’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.334. (b) asked The given passage states that aunt Jenny asked him questions that have been full of deceit. Hence ‘asked’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.335. (b) talent ‘Talent’ means a natural ability or skill in doing something. The given passage states that It seemed as if she was endowed with a talent which could hunt for dark and mysterious tact. Hence ‘talent’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.336. (d) suspicion ‘Suspicion’ means a feeling or belief that someone is not telling the truth. The given passage states that Paul felt an uncomfortable suspicion. Hence ‘suspicion’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.337. (c) searched ‘Search’ means to look somewhere carefully in order to find something. The given passage states that he searched Aunt Jennys face. Hence, ‘searched’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.338. (a) sowed The idiom ‘sow the seeds’ means to cause an idea or feeling to be in someone’s mind. The given passage states that the early nationalists sowed the seeds of Indian nationalism well and deep. Hence, 'sowed' is the most appropriate answer. Sol.339. (a) shallow ‘Shallow’ means not deep or strong. The given passage states that the early nationalists did not base their nationalism or appeals to shallow sentiments and passing emotions, or abstract rights of freedom and liberty, or on obscurantist appeals to the past. Hence, ‘shallow’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.340. (a) rooted ‘Root’ means to establish deeply and firmly. The given passage states that they rooted it in a hard-headed and penetrating analysis of the complex mechanism of modern imperialism and the chief contradiction between the interests of the Indian people and British rule. Hence, 'rooted' is the most appropriate answer. Sol.341. (b) evolved ‘Evolved’ means to develop gradually. The given passage states that they evolved a common political and economic programme. Hence, 'evolved' is the most appropriate answer. Sol.342. (b) united ‘Unite’ means to bring together a group of people. The given passage states that they evolved a common political and economic programme which united rather than divided the different sections of the people. Hence, ‘united’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.343. (a) a Indefinite article ‘a/an’ is used before a singular noun. Article ‘a’ is used before a word beginning with a consonant sound. For example:- a cat, a new car, etc. Similarly, in the given sentence, ‘slave’ is a singular noun and the word ‘very’ begins with the consonant sound. Hence, ‘a’ is the most appropriate answer. Cloze Test Sol.344. (a) to gain The main verb (ran away) is already there in the sentence. So, we need a non-finite verb. Infinitive (to + V1) is non-finite that acts as a noun, adjective or adverb in a sentence. Similarly, in the given sentence, ‘to gain’ is an infinitive acting as an adverb of reason. Hence, ‘to gain’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.345. (a) roaring Present participle (V-ing) is used to indicate an action happening at the same time as another. Similarly, the lion was roaring with pain when he saw it. Hence, ‘roaring’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.346. (b) them ‘Them’ is a third person pronoun that is used to talk about two or more people or things other than the person speaking and the person listening. In the given sentence, ‘them’ has been used for the lion and the slave. Hence, ‘them’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.347. (b) be The given sentence is in the passive voice and the correct structure is ‘Let + be + V3’. Hence, ‘be’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.348. (d) Numerous ‘Numerous’ means many or a lot of.The given passage talks about numerous health benefits, including protection from cardiovascular disease and cancer. Hence ‘numerous’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.349. (b) attributed ‘Attributed’ means something is caused by or as a result of someone or something. The given passage states numerous health benefits, including protection from cardiovascular disease and cancer, have been attributed to antioxidants. Hence ‘attributed’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.350. (b) utilised ‘Uitilise’ means to make use of something effectively. The given passage states that these benefits depend on how well antioxidants are absorbed and utilised by the body. Hence, ‘utilised’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.351. (c) analysed ‘Analysed’ means to examine something in detail. The given passage states that dates had the highest antioxidants per serving out of all the foods and drinks analysed. Hence ‘analysed’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.352. (a) top ‘Top’ means the highest part of something. The given passage states that coffee comes out on top, according to Vinson. Hence ‘top’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.353. (b) believed ‘Believed’ means held as an opinion. The given passage states that it is generally believed that the methodology of teaching-learning is the concern of teachers only. Hence, ‘believed’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.354. (b) little ‘Little’ means almost nothing. The given passage states that learners have little or nothing to do with it. Hence, ‘little’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.355. (d) methods ‘Method’ is a particular way of doing something. The given passage states that the teaching-learning methods are concerns of learners as well. Hence, ‘methods’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.356. (b) relevance ‘Relevance’ means the state of being closely connected or appropriate. The given passage states that it has more relevance for the learners of physical education. Hence, ‘relevance’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.357. (c) merely ‘Merely’ means just. The given passage states that we mean that you have actually participated in the subject area, rather than merely studied it. Hence, ‘merely’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.358. (c) living The given passage states that it is called that because it is the only living reptile in a group of reptiles that dates back about 220 million years to the time of the dinosaurs. Hence ‘living’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.359. (b) develops ‘Develops’ means to expand or grow with time. The given passage states that it develops to a length of around 60 cm. Hence ‘develops’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.360. (b) intriguing ‘Intriguing’ means something that is very interesting. The given passage states that the fact that this lizard has three eyes is its most intriguing feature. Hence ‘intriguing’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.361. (c) distinct ‘Distinct’ means something that is clearly different. The given passage states that the tuatara has teeth that are distinct from those of other reptiles. Hence ‘distinct’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.362. (a) fertilised ‘Fertilise’ means to develop into a new young animal or plant. The given passage states that these eggs are fertilised during summer. Hence ‘fertilised’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.363. (d) dissolve ‘Dissolve’ means to be absorbed by a liquid, especially when mixed. The given passage states that oils are liquids that easily burn and do not dissolve in water. Hence ‘dissolve’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.364. (d) primary ‘Primary’ means of chief importance. The given passage states that there are three primary types of oils: mineral, essential and fixed oils. Hence ‘primary’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.365. (c) utilised ‘Utilised’ means to use something effectively. The given passage states that essential oils are utilised in perfumery and food flavouring. Hence ‘utilised’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.366. (d) produced Cloze Test ‘Produced’ means make or manufacture from raw materials. The given passage states that mineral oils are produced from petroleum which has been underground for millions of years from sea organism skeletons. Hence ‘produced’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.367. (b) combination ‘Combination’ means a mixture of two or more things. The given passage states that they are made up of a combination of hydrocarbons. Hence ‘combination’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.368. (a) who ‘Who’ is used when the antecedent is the doer of the action. In the given sentence, the antecedent is ‘man’, who is the doer of the action ‘telling the fortunes’. Hence, ‘who’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.369. (c) has The part within quotes will be written in the present perfect tense as these are the exact words of the speaker. ‘Somebody’ is a singular pronoun that will take ‘has’ as the singular verb. Hence, ‘has’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.370. (a) quite The given sentence needs an adverb to modify the meaning of the adjective ‘shocked’. ‘Quite’ is an adverb which means completely. Hence, ‘quite’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.371. (d) commented The given sentence is in the past tense so it will have the verb in the past form(V2). Hence, ‘commented(V2)’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.372. (c) tells The part within quotes will be written in the present tense as these are the exact words of the speaker. In the given sentence, ‘he’ is a singular subject that will take ‘tells’ as a singular verb. Hence, ‘tells’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.373. (b) caused ‘Caused’ means to make something happen. The given passage states that famines were caused during the period of British rule due to many reasons. Hence ‘caused’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.374. (b) including large-scale ‘Including’ is used to introduce some examples of things that we are talking about. ‘Large-scale’ means involving many people. The given passage introduces some examples of reasons for famines. ‘Large-scale unemployment’ is one of them. Hence, ‘including large-scale’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.375. (a) restricted ‘Restricted’ means limited in quantity. The given passage states some reasons for famines which include large-scale unemployment following import of machine-made goods from Britain, overexploitation of farmers and restricted access to food. Hence, ‘restricted’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.376. (d) self-sufficiency ‘Self-sufficiency’ means the quality of being able to maintain oneself without outside support. The given passage states that India achieved self-sufficiency in food grains by the year 1976. Hence, ‘self-sufficiency’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.377. (c) Green revolution ‘Green revolution’ is the great increase in the production of food grains. The given passage states that India achieved self-sufficiency in food grains by the year 1956 through the implementation of the seed-water-fertiliser-led green revolution. Hence, ‘Green revolution’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.378. (d) conclusion ‘Conclusion’ means a judgement or decision reached by reasoning. The given passage states that nutrition experts have researched the bad effects of junk food and have come to the conclusion that junk food manufacturing companies are fooling the people. Hence, ‘conclusion’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.379. (b) showing The given passage states that nutrition experts have researched the bad effects of junk food and have come to the conclusion that junk food manufacturing companies are fooling the people by showing deceptive advertisements that show junk food as healthy. Hence, ‘showing’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.380. (b) healthier ‘Healthier’ means providing better nutrition. The given passage states that we must substitute junk food with healthier food like fruits and vegetables. Hence, ‘healthier’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.381. (d) grabbing ‘Grabbing’ means to take something in one’s hand. The given passage states that the next time you think of grabbing that burger, hotdog, pizza, samosa, kachori or even ice cream in a restaurant, eating joint or a party, stop your instincts and look for some healthier food options. Hence, ‘grabbing’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.382. (b) options The given passage states that the next time you think of grabbing that burger, hotdog, pizza, samosa, kachori or even ice cream in a restaurant, eating joint or a party, stop your instincts and look for some healthier food options instead. Hence, ‘options’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.383. (d) in ‘In diameter’ is the correct phrase to indicate the measurement of the size of the Sun considered as a sphere. Hence, ‘in’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.384. (a) regions ‘Regions’ means specific areas or parts of a place. The given passage states that sunspots are dreary regions on the Sun's surface that are two thousand degrees Celsius cooler than the rest of the surface. Hence ‘regions’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.385. (c) appear ‘Appear’ means to give the impression of doing something, though not actually happening. The given passage states that these groups appear to travel across the Sun over two weeks as the Sun rotates. Hence ‘appear’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.386. (a) refers ‘Refers to’ means to denote something. The given passage states that the solar or sunspot cycle refers to this. Hence ‘refers’ is the most appropriate answer. Cloze Test Sol.387. (b) observations ‘Observation’ means the recorded information that results from studying a scientific event. The given passage states that according to the observations made by satellites like Nimbus 7, when sunspots are at their largest, less heat reaches Earth. Hence ‘observations’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.388. (c) widely ‘Widely’ means by a large group of people. The given passage states that the Big Bang theory has been the most widely accepted. Hence, ‘widely’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.389. (d) form ‘Form’ means to combine to create something. The given passage states that this singularity contained all the matter and energy that would eventually form the stars, galaxies and other structures that make up our universe today. Hence, ‘form’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.390. (a) wealth ‘Wealth’ means a large amount. The given passage states that the Big Bang theory is supported by a wealth of observational evidence. Hence, 'wealth' is the most appropriate answer. Sol.391. (c) abundance ‘Abundance’ means a large quantity of something. The given passage states that the Big Bang theory is supported by a wealth of observational evidence, including the cosmic microwave background radiation, the abundance of light elements in the universe and the large-scale structure of the cosmos. Hence, ‘abundance’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.392. (d) nature ‘Nature’ means the basic constitution of a thing. The given passage states that the Big Bang theory is still an active area of research, with scientists seeking to better understand the earliest moments of the universe and the nature of dark matter and energy. Hence, ‘nature’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.393. (d) characterised ‘Characterised’ means to describe the quality of something. The given passage states that it is characterised by an emphasis on emotion, imagination and individualism. Hence ‘characterised’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.394. (d) themes ‘Theme’ means an idea that recurs in a work of art or literature. The given passage states that romantic poets sought to express their innermost thoughts and feelings through their poetry, often exploring themes such as love, beauty and mortality. Hence ‘themes’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.395. (c) believed The given passage is expressing a belief of romantic poets in the past. Therefore, past form(V2) of the verb will be used to indicate the past tense. Hence ‘believed(V2)’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.396. (b) another ‘Another’ means one more. The given passage states that another important aspect of Romantic poetry is its focus on the individual and the subjective experience. Hence ‘another’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.397. (d) as well as ‘As well as’ is used to mention another item connected with the subject after conjunction ‘and’. The given passage states that this led to a greater emphasis on personal expression and introspection in poetry, as well as a rejection of the traditional poetic forms and structures of the past. Hence ‘as well as’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.398. (d) complex ‘Complex’ means consisting of many different and connected parts. The given passage states that hierarchy is key in ensuring normal functioning of our increasingly complex society. Hence, ‘complex’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.399.

  1. diversity ‘Diversity’ means many different types of things. The given passage states that in the US, there is an enormous variety of other domains of human activity or interests. Hence, ‘diversity’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.400.
  2. especially ‘Especially’ means more in particular circumstances than others. The given passage states that leadership evolves as a necessity almost in any realm of human activity/thinking, especially where more people are involved. Hence, ‘especially’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.401.
  3. accepting ‘Accepting’ means believing that something is true. The given passage states that it isn’t hard accepting why even in academic institutions throughout the US and worldwide, students are given leadership essay assignments. Hence, ‘accepting’ is the most appropriate answer. Sol.402.
  4. familiar ‘Familiar’ means knowing something very well. The given passage states that most students will take on leadership roles with various degrees of responsibility and impact in their lives, hence, becoming familiar with typical challenges,
